compiled_state.title: As a practitioner, /dso:sprint drains autonomous work first then pauses in-session on manual-tagged stories with a verification handshake and completion-verifier sentinel
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Teach /dso:sprint to recognize the manual:awaiting_user tag: filter such stories out of the autonomous batch, drain all unblocked autonomous stories first, then present a single blocking in-session prompt listing per-story instructions and verification commands. Accept inputs done, done <story-id>, and skip. On done with a verification_command present, execute it in a constrained subshell with a timeout and proceed or re-prompt based on exit code; on done without a verification_command, require a user-typed confirmation token and log it as a ticket comment. Write a structured sentinel (audit token + verification_command exit code) to the ticket as a comment so dso:completion-verifier can close manual stories at Step 10a without attempting to re-execute the manual step. Topological sort surfaces manual stories before any transitive autonomous dependents; cycles involving manual stories are detected; skip propagation to transitive dependents is documented and applied.

**Why**: Advances SC5. This is the user-facing handshake that converts the tag into a lived behavior. Without it, the tag is just metadata.

**Scope**:
- IN: Tag filter for autonomous batch (generalized with design:awaiting_import via OR); topological-sort adjustment covering transitive dependents, cycle detection, and skip-propagation semantics; blocking in-session prompt rendering instructions + verification_command + audit-token requirement per story; input handling for done / done <story-id> / skip; verification_command execution (pre-execution dangerous-pattern re-check, pre-execution command-text logging to ticket, constrained subshell, length cap, configurable timeout); confirmation-token collection and audit-comment writing; structured sentinel comment (audit_token + verification_command exit code + user input) written as ticket comment for completion-verifier consumption; completion-verifier read-path update so manual:awaiting_user stories close at Step 10a based on the sentinel. Flag-gated by planning.external_dependency_block_enabled.
- OUT: State persistence across session kills (Story I handles resilience); brainstorm-side token marker generation (Story C); preplanning-side tag emission (Story D); implementation-plan-side prep decomposition (Story E).

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sprint filters manual:awaiting_user-tagged stories out of the autonomous batch and completes all unblocked autonomous stories first
  Satisfies: "/dso:sprint drains autonomous work first, then pauses in-session on manual stories. Stories tagged manual:awaiting_user are filtered from the autonomous batch."
- When this story is complete, when only manual-tagged stories remain, sprint presents a single blocking prompt with per-story instructions and verification commands; inputs done / done <story-id> / skip are accepted and dispatched correctly
  Satisfies: "sprint presents a single blocking prompt listing per-story instructions + verification_command. Accepts done, done <story-id>, and skip"
- When this story is complete, verification_command execution re-checks dangerous patterns at execution time, logs the full command text to the ticket before execution, runs in a constrained subshell, rejects commands exceeding a length cap, and enforces a configurable timeout; sprint proceeds on pass (exit 0) and re-prompts with failure output on non-zero exit or timeout
  Satisfies: "on done with a verification_command present, sprint runs it and proceeds on pass or re-prompts with failure output on fail"
- When this story is complete, sprint accepts a user-typed confirmation token when no verification_command is present, writes the token to the ticket as an audit comment, and proceeds
  Satisfies: "on done with no verification_command, sprint requires a confirmation token and logs it."
- When this story is complete, sprint writes a structured sentinel comment (audit_token + verification_command exit code + user input) to each manual story ticket after handshake, and dso:completion-verifier closes manual:awaiting_user stories at Step 10a by reading the sentinel (not by re-executing the manual step)
  Satisfies: "sprint session on a mixed epic reaches the manual pause after autonomous work, handles each input variant correctly, and resumes"
- When this story is complete, topological sort surfaces manual stories before all transitive autonomous dependents (M blocks A blocks B → M is surfaced before both); cycles involving manual stories are detected with a clear diagnostic; skip propagation marks transitive dependents as waiting-on-skipped with diagnostic output
  Satisfies: "Topological sort surfaces manual stories before any dependents so autonomous work is not blocked on a user who has not been prompted yet."
- When this story is complete, with the flag off the tag is ignored and sprint behaves identically to pre-epic baseline (no filter, no pause, no handshake)
  Satisfies: "The entire feature sits behind a config flag ... When off, all four skills behave identically to current baseline."
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for: tag filter, topo-sort with transitive dependents + cycles + skip propagation, handshake input handling (all three variants), verification_command execution (pass / fail / timeout / dangerous-pattern rejection / oversize rejection), confirmation-token collection + audit write, sentinel write, completion-verifier sentinel-read path

## Considerations

- [Security] verification_command is user-authored shell; render-time warnings in Story B are advisory only. Sprint must re-validate at execution time because the epic description is editable between brainstorm and sprint; log command text pre-execution for audit; run in constrained subshell (no source, no alias); cap command length
- [Consumer-impact] completion-verifier (Never-Do rule 23) is the mandatory closure path. Sprint's sentinel + completion-verifier's sentinel-read logic must land together in this story so Step 10a can close manual stories without regression
- [Testing] Interactive blocking prompt needs a mock-compatible seam (env var or file-based input) so automated tests exercise done/skip/done <id> without a live terminal
- [Reliability] Cycles involving manual stories and cycles between design:awaiting_import stories and manual:awaiting_user stories must both be detected; diagnostic should name the cycle participants
- [Performance] verification_command default timeout 30s (configurable via planning.verification_command_timeout_seconds); commands exceeding it are failed with clear diagnostic
- [Maintainability] Tag-filter logic should be generalized to support multiple awaiting_* tags (manual:awaiting_user, design:awaiting_import, design:awaiting_review) via a shared predicate; a story with any awaiting_* tag is held out of the autonomous batch

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: VERIFICATION_RESULT: pass — all 8 done definitions verified by dso:completion-verifier. Step 3b boundary test gap remediated via task dfa1-c877.
comments.author: Test
